overview: Physics Sim is a browser-based MuJoCo rigid-body physics simulator. Scenes are defined as a tree of SceneNode bodies, each with primitive geoms and joints. The simulator compiles scenes to MJCF XML and runs MuJoCo WASM in the browser. Coordinate system: X=right, Y=forward/depth, Z=up. Ground plane at Z=0.
workflow: 1. Call physics_get_schema to understand available geom/joint types and fields.
2. Use physics_build_scene (easy, auto-fills defaults) or physics_update_scene (full control).

3. Use physics_run_headless to simulate N ticks and verify behaviour programmatically — check final positions, velocities, or trajectory shape against expected values. Do this before relying on visual inspection.
4. Call physics_play to show the simulation to the user once you have confirmed it behaves correctly via headless run.
5. Use physics_set_environment to adjust gravity, wind, floor friction, and fluid density.
gotchas: DO NOT add a body with a plane geom as a floor. MuJoCo provides a built-in ground plane at Z=0 automatically. Adding a plane body causes a silent compile error — the scene loads but physics_play returns ok:true while isPlaying stays false. Omit the floor body entirely.

After calling physics_play, immediately call physics_get_state and check that isPlaying is true. If it is still false, the scene failed to compile (likely a bad geom, duplicate name, or plane body conflict). Fix the scene and try again.
Script functions require the 'api.' prefix. Writing getTime() instead of api.getTime() throws ReferenceError and silently breaks the script. All api calls must be api.functionName(...).
To give a body an initial velocity, use a script with a first-tick guard: if (api.getTime() === 0) { api.setVelocity([...], 'bodyName'); }. Using === 0 fires exactly on the first tick.

The script field on a SceneNode is a single string: 'script': '...' — NOT an array. Passing scripts:[{code:'...'}] or scripts:['...'] will silently be ignored and the script will never run. Always use the singular 'script' key with a flat string value.

Bodies that should NOT collide with each other (e.g. windmill blades, rotor hub, nacelle) MUST have contype:0 AND conaffinity:0 on ALL their geoms. Without this, sibling bodies that overlap near a shared parent origin generate huge contact-constraint forces that oscillate and prevent scripted torques from accumulating. Even a few millimetres of geom overlap near the hub causes ±1 rad/s oscillation that prevents spin-up.

api.getOrientation() returns the 9-element row-major rotation matrix of the body the script is attached to (xmat). Column 0 = [R[0], R[3], R[6]] is the body's local X-axis in world space. For a rotor with hinge axis=[1,0,0], project wind onto this axis to get the effective wind speed: Vw = windX*R[0] + windY*R[3].

coordinateSystem.mujocoWorldSpace: X=right, Y=forward (into screen), Z=up. Ground plane at Z=0. Gravity default: Z=-9.81.
coordinateSystem.geomPos: pos field on geoms is a local offset from the body origin in MuJoCo Z-up space.
coordinateSystem.bodyPos: pos field on bodies is relative to parent body (or world origin for root bodies).
coordinateSystem.meshVertices: EXCEPTION: mesh geom vertices are in Three.js Y-up world space (X=right, Y=up, Z=toward camera). See mesh section.

tools.physics_get_state: Return full Physics Sim state: sceneGraph, isPlaying, isLoaded, gravity, wind, density.
tools.physics_get_scene: Return the scene graph (bodies, joints, geoms, scripts).
tools.physics_toggle_play: Toggle play/pause of the physics simulation.
tools.physics_reset: Reset the physics simulation to its initial state.
tools.physics_list_presets: Return available physics scene preset names.
tools.physics_load_preset: Load a physics scene preset. Valid presets: pendulum, cubes, gears, machine, rack_pinion, inclined_plane, pulley_system, cartpole, newtons_cradle, suspension_bridge, paper_plane, monkey_head, golden_gate, golden_gate_mesh, mesh_collision, coin_flip, windmill, physics_only_windmill, traditional_windmill.
tools.physics_set_environment: Update physics environment. gravityZ is negative for downward gravity (e.g. -9.81). windX/windY add lateral fluid drag. density adds buoyancy. floorFriction controls ground grip. All fields optional.
tools.physics_update_scene: Replace the full scene graph. Must match the SceneGraph type: { nodes: SceneNode[] }. Full control but requires complete valid structure.
tools.physics_get_schema: Return the full schema describing every geom type, joint type, and field. Call this first when building scenes.
tools.physics_build_scene: Build and load a scene from simplified body descriptors. Auto-fills safe defaults. Easier than physics_update_scene.
tools.physics_run_headless: Run the physics simulation headlessly for N ticks. Returns trajectory: [{time, bodies:{name:{pos,vel,angvel}}, joints:{name:{pos,vel}}}]. Does not affect the live view. Use this to verify scene behaviour programmatically — e.g. check a projectile lands in the right place, a pendulum reaches the right angle, or a body comes to rest at the expected position — before calling physics_play for visual presentation.

geomSizes.box: half-extents [hx, hy, hz] — full dimensions are 2*hx × 2*hy × 2*hz
geomSizes.capsule: [radius, half-height] — a cylinder of half-height hh capped with hemispheres of radius r. Or use fromto to specify endpoints directly.
geomSizes.ellipsoid: semi-axes [rx, ry, rz] — like a sphere stretched independently on each axis
geomSizes.plane: ignored — MuJoCo treats it as an infinite ground plane
geomSizes.mesh: not used — shape defined by vertices+faces. VISUAL ONLY by default — does not simulate or collide.

jointTypes.free: 6-DOF unconstrained — body falls freely under gravity. Use for dropped objects.
jointTypes.hinge: 1-DOF rotation around axis. Specify axis:[x,y,z]. Use for wheels, doors, pendulums.
jointTypes.slide: 1-DOF translation along axis. Specify axis:[x,y,z]. Use for pistons, rails.
jointTypes.ball: 3-DOF rotation (ball-and-socket). Use with stiffness+damping for springs, sway.

geomFields.name: string — unique identifier (required)
geomFields.pos: [x,y,z] — local offset from body origin in MuJoCo Z-up space
geomFields.euler: [roll,pitch,yaw] degrees — local rotation, alternative to quat
geomFields.fromto: [x1,y1,z1, x2,y2,z2] — capsule/cylinder endpoints in local space, overrides size/pos/quat
geomFields.mass: number — override density-based mass for this geom
geomFields.contype: integer bitmask — collision group this geom belongs to
geomFields.conaffinity: integer bitmask — which groups this geom collides with
geomFields.vertices: number[] — mesh only: flat [x0,y0,z0,...] in Three.js Y-up space (X=right, Y=up, Z=toward camera)
geomFields.faces: number[] — mesh only: flat triangle indices [i0,j0,k0,...] with outward-facing (CCW) winding
geomFields.dynamic: boolean — if true, mesh participates fully in simulation and collision (convex hull). Requires renderVertices.
geomFields.renderVertices: number[] — dynamic mesh only: flat [x0,y0,z0,...] in raw MuJoCo Z-up space. Convert from Y-up: (x,y,z)→(x,-z,y). Do NOT subtract centroid — MuJoCo recenters internally and xpos tracks the result.
geomFields.margin: number — expand contact surface outward by this amount (meters). Useful to prevent sinking.
geomFields.gap: number — dead zone inside margin where no contact force is applied.

nodeFields.name: string — display name and MuJoCo XML name
nodeFields.pos: [x,y,z] — position relative to parent (or world for root)
nodeFields.euler: [roll,pitch,yaw] degrees — body orientation, alternative to quat
nodeFields.geoms: SceneGeom[] — one or more geoms defining shape
nodeFields.joints: SceneJoint[] — joints to parent (empty = fixed to world)
nodeFields.coupleTargetId: id of body to gear-couple first joints with
nodeFields.weldTargetId: id of body to rigidly weld (closed-loop constraint)
nodeFields.connectTargetId: id of body for ball-socket point constraint
nodeFields.script: JavaScript control script running at 1000 Hz. IMPORTANT: all functions must be called with the 'api.' prefix — e.g. api.getTime(), api.setVelocity([...], 'bodyName'). Bare calls like getTime() will throw ReferenceError. Available: api.isKeyPressed(keyName), api.getPosition(bodyName), api.getVelocity(bodyName), api.getAngularVelocity(bodyName), api.setPosition(pos, bodyName), api.setVelocity(vel, bodyName), api.setAngularVelocity(angvel, bodyName), api.getMass(bodyName), api.getJointPosition(jointName), api.getJointVelocity(jointName), api.applyForce(forceVec, bodyName), api.applyTorque(torqueVec, bodyName), api.applyJointForce(jointName, forceVal), api.setActuatorControl(actuatorName, ctrlVal), api.getTime(), api.getWind(), api.log(msg). To launch a body with an initial velocity on the first tick: 'if (api.getTime() === 0) { api.setVelocity([vx, vy, vz], "bodyName"); api.setAngularVelocity([wx, wy, wz], "bodyName"); }'

jointFields.name: string — unique joint name
jointFields.pos: [x,y,z] — joint anchor point in local body space
jointFields.damping: number — velocity-proportional resistance (for free joints, scales drag force/torque to decay velocity and spin uniformly)
jointFields.stiffness: number — spring constant pulling back to springref
jointFields.limited: boolean — whether range limits are active
jointFields.range: [min,max] — joint limits in degrees (hinge) or meters (slide)

meshNotes.staticMesh: No dynamic field (or dynamic:false). VISUAL ONLY — does not simulate, move, or collide. Vertices in Three.js Y-up world space. Good for scenery, decorative structures, visual shells around primitive collision proxies.
meshNotes.dynamicMesh: dynamic:true. Full physics + collision (convex hull). Vertices in Three.js Y-up for collision, renderVertices in raw MuJoCo Z-up for rendering. Body moves with simulation.
meshNotes.renderVerticesRule: renderVertices = Y-up vertices with Y↔Z swapped: (x,y,z)→(x,-z,y). No centroid subtraction. MuJoCo recenters the mesh internally; xpos tracks the body frame correctly.
meshNotes.faceWinding: Use outward-facing CCW winding. Wrong winding causes inside-out contact normals and objects sinking through surfaces.
meshNotes.bodyPos: For a dynamic mesh whose Y-up base is at Y=0, set body_pos=[0,0,0]. The mesh will render with its MuJoCo origin at ground level. Adjust body_pos.z to raise/lower.
goodUseCases: Dynamic convex polyhedra (pyramids, crystals, custom shapes)

examples.title: Falling Sphere
examples.description: A single sphere dropped from height 3m with a free joint. Simplest possible simulation.

examples.title: Stack of Boxes
examples.description: Three boxes stacked on top of each other, each with a free joint so they can topple.

examples.title: Simple Pendulum
examples.description: A rod with a heavy bob hanging from a fixed pivot. Hinge joint on Y-axis.

examples.title: Spinning Motor
examples.description: A cylinder with a velocity-actuated hinge motor spinning at 2 rad/s.

examples.title: Compound Weird Shape
examples.description: An asymmetric compound body built from multiple geom types on one body — demonstrates the compound geom approach for arbitrary shapes.

examples.title: Wind-Responsive Structure
examples.description: A tall mast on a stiff ball joint that sways in wind and springs back. Set windX=5 in environment to see it sway.
examples.setup: First call: physics_set_environment(windX=5.0). Then load the scene.

examples.title: Headless Trajectory Analysis
examples.description: Run a pendulum simulation for 500 ticks and analyse the trajectory without disrupting the live view.
steps: # Analyse max angle: max(abs(t['joints']['hinge']['pos']) for t in result.trajectory)
# Period: find zero crossings in joints.hinge.pos

examples.title: Chained Pendulum (Multi-body)
examples.description: A double pendulum: pole1 hangs from world, pole2 hangs from the tip of pole1. Demonstrates parent-child body tree.

examples.title: Gear Coupling
examples.description: Two meshing gears coupled via explicit joint coupling. One is motor-driven; the other counter-rotates via coupleTargetId.

examples.title: Static Mesh Scenery (Visual Only)
examples.description: A decorative arch made from mesh geoms. Fixed to world (no joint). VISUAL ONLY — does not simulate or collide. Use primitives if you need physics.
examples.note: Mesh vertices are in Three.js Y-up world space: X=right, Y=up, Z=toward camera. Ground at Y=0.

examples.title: Scripted Launch — Coin Flip into Cup
examples.description: A gold coin launched off the floor with an initial velocity via a first-tick script. Demonstrates api.setVelocity / api.setAngularVelocity and building a hollow cup from fixed box walls. No floor body needed — MuJoCo's built-in ground plane is used.
notes: All script calls use api.* prefix.
The time guard (api.getTime() < 0.003) fires only on the first simulation step.
Cup walls are separate fixed bodies (joints:[]) — no joint means fixed to world.
Trajectory maths: vx=0.4, vz=5.0 → coin reaches x=0.4m at t≈1s, drops through cup opening at z=0.06m.

examples.title: Wind Simulation — Bridge Sway
examples.description: Load the Golden Gate Bridge preset (which has a ball-jointed deck) and apply wind to make it sway.
steps: # The deck and cables will sway laterally and spring back.

examples.title: BEM Aerodynamic Rotor Script
examples.description: Blade Element Momentum script for a wind-driven rotor hinge. Attach this script to the rotor body. Set windX via physics_set_environment. All blade and hub geoms must have contype:0/conaffinity:0 to prevent contact oscillation. The traditional_windmill preset uses this pattern with 4 blades.
notes: api.getOrientation() returns the 9-element xmat for the script's own body. Column 0 = [R[0],R[3],R[6]] = rotor X-axis in world (the hinge axis direction).
Project wind onto the rotor axis: Vw = windX*R[0] + windY*R[3]. This is the effective axial wind speed.
BEM integrates lift/drag over N radial segments. Tangential force × radius × numBlades gives total hinge torque.
Apply with api.applyJointForce('rotor_hinge', totalTorque). Forces reset to zero each tick before scripts run.
With windX=5, R_blade=0.9, 4 blades, pitch=18°: rotor reaches ~-1.9 rad/s at t=0.5s.
